claimTypes.title: Life Insurance Death Claim
claimTypes.intro: A death claim is made by the nominee when the life insured passes away during the policy term. IRDAI mandates settlement within 30 days of receiving all documents.

documents.note: Physical copy or e-policy printout
Issued by Municipal Corporation / Gram Panchayat — get minimum 5 certified copies
Aadhaar / PAN / Passport of nominee
Available on insurer website or branch — fill carefully
In nominee's name for direct bank transfer
Hospital records, doctor's certificate, discharge summary
FIR copy + post-mortem report for accidental deaths
Confirming employment and income details

steps.title: Notify the Insurer Immediately
steps.detail: Call the insurer's claim helpline or visit the nearest branch as soon as possible. Most insurers have a 24×7 toll-free number. Get a claim reference number.

steps.title: Collect the Claim Form
steps.detail: Download Form A (claimant statement) from the insurer's website or collect from a branch. Fill all fields accurately — errors cause delays.

steps.title: Arrange All Documents
steps.detail: Gather all required documents. Get multiple certified copies of the death certificate — you will need them for multiple submissions including bank, EPF, and insurance.

steps.title: Submit Documents to Insurer
steps.detail: Submit at the nearest branch or send via registered post/courier. Keep proof of submission. Many insurers now accept digital submissions through their app.

steps.title: Claim Investigation (if applicable)
steps.detail: For claims in the first 3 years (contestability period), the insurer may conduct an investigation. Cooperate fully — this typically takes 5–10 working days.

steps.title: Claim Settlement
steps.detail: After all documents are verified, the insurer must settle within 30 days (IRDAI mandate). Amount is credited directly to the nominee's bank account via NEFT.

claimTypes.timeline: IRDAI mandates: 30 days from receipt of all documents. For investigated claims: 45 days. If delayed beyond this, the insurer must pay interest at 2% above bank rate.
mistakes: Not informing family about the insurance policy — nominee doesn't know a claim exists
Wrong or outdated nominee details in the policy
Not collecting enough copies of the death certificate
Submitting photocopies instead of originals (ask insurer what they accept)
Missing the free-look period on lapsed policy (check revival status before death)
Not following up — silence does not mean approval
tips: Store policy document + nominee details in a shared family folder (physical and digital)
Save the insurer's claims helpline in your phone contacts
Inform your nominee about the policy existence, UIN, and insurer details
Buy early — claims in year 1 or 2 face more scrutiny than seasoned policies
Never hide medical history — claim rejection due to non-disclosure is legally valid
IRDAI Grievance: If claim wrongly rejected, file at bimabharosa.irdai.gov.in

claimTypes.title: Health Insurance Claim
claimTypes.intro: Health insurance claims are either Cashless (direct settlement at network hospital) or Reimbursement (you pay first, insurer pays back). Knowing the process ensures faster settlement.

subtypes.title: Cashless Claim (Network Hospital)

documents.note: Download from insurer app before hospitalization
Aadhaar / PAN of patient
Filled by treating doctor and hospital
Written recommendation for admission

steps.title: Inform Insurer / TPA
steps.detail: For planned hospitalization: inform at least 48–72 hours in advance. For emergencies: inform within 24 hours of admission. Call the TPA's 24×7 helpline.

steps.title: Go to Network Hospital
steps.detail: Visit the insurer's empanelled (network) hospital. Non-network hospitals require reimbursement. Check the network list on the insurer's website or app.

steps.title: Show Insurance Card at Hospital's TPA Desk
steps.detail: The hospital's insurance desk handles the process. Show your health card, policy number, and photo ID. They fill the pre-authorization form.

steps.title: Pre-Authorization Approval
steps.detail: The hospital sends the pre-auth form to TPA. TPA reviews coverage and approves the estimated treatment cost — typically within 2–4 hours for planned cases, faster for emergencies.

steps.title: Get Treated
steps.detail: Receive treatment. The insurer covers admissible expenses. Non-admissible expenses (e.g., personal comfort items, administrative charges) are paid by you at discharge.

steps.title: Discharge and Final Settlement
steps.detail: Hospital submits final bills to TPA. Insurer settles directly with the hospital. You only pay the non-admissible amounts and any co-payment as per your policy.

subtypes.timeline: Pre-authorization: 2–4 hours (planned), 30 minutes (emergency). Final hospital settlement: 2–7 working days after discharge.
mistakes: Going to a non-network hospital for planned treatment and expecting cashless
Not informing the TPA before or immediately after admission
Not carrying the health insurance card during hospitalization
Expecting 100% settlement — non-admissible expenses are always excluded
tips: Download your insurer's app and e-health card before you need it
Save TPA helpline number in your phone contacts as 'Health Insurance TPA'
Check if your preferred hospital is in the network annually — hospitals exit networks
Read your policy's list of non-admissible expenses to avoid bill shock at discharge

subtypes.title: Reimbursement Claim (Any Hospital)

documents.note: Download from insurer website
Itemized — cannot be replaced, keep safely
Doctor's summary of treatment and diagnosis
All prescriptions issued during treatment
Lab reports, X-rays, scan reports with radiologist's note
Aadhaar / PAN
For direct bank transfer
For ported or renewed policies

steps.title: Inform Insurer
steps.detail: Notify the insurer/TPA of hospitalization as soon as possible, even for reimbursement claims. Many policies require notification within 24–48 hours of admission.

steps.title: Get Treated and Collect ALL Documents
steps.detail: Collect every receipt, bill, prescription, and report — originals, not photocopies. Once you leave the hospital, getting duplicate originals is very difficult.

steps.title: Fill the Claim Form Carefully
steps.detail: Download the claim form from the insurer's website. Fill all fields accurately — diagnosis, treatment dates, hospital name, doctor's name. Errors delay processing.

steps.title: Submit Within Deadline
steps.detail: Submit all documents within the stipulated time (typically 30 days of discharge). Late submissions can be rejected. Submit at branch or via registered courier with proof.

steps.title: TPA Review and Queries
steps.detail: TPA reviews documents and may ask for additional information (clarification on diagnosis, missing reports). Respond promptly to avoid delays.

steps.title: Settlement
steps.detail: After satisfactory verification, amount is credited to your bank account (minus deductibles, co-payment, non-admissible expenses) within 30 days of document receipt.

subtypes.timeline: Settlement within 30 days of receipt of all documents (IRDAI mandate). Interest applies if delayed without reason.
mistakes: Losing original bills — photocopies are not accepted for most insurers
Missing the submission deadline (usually 30 days from discharge)
Submitting incomplete forms — always double-check before submitting
Not keeping copies of everything submitted — you may need them later
tips: Photograph all bills and reports before submitting originals — keep digital backups
Use registered post or courier with tracking for document submission
Follow up every week after submission — don't assume silence means processing
If claim is under-settled, you can approach IRDAI or Insurance Ombudsman

claimTypes.title: Travel Insurance Claim
claimTypes.intro: Travel insurance covers medical emergencies abroad, trip cancellation, baggage loss, and flight delays. Speed of notification is critical — most claims must be reported within 24 hours of incident.

documents.note: Digital copy on phone is sufficient abroad
Photo page and visa stamps
Proof of travel dates
For medical emergency claims — must be in English or translated
For theft, baggage loss, or passport loss
From airline counter for delay / lost baggage claims
From tour operator for trip cancellation claims
For settlement in your bank account

steps.title: Save the Emergency Helpline BEFORE Travelling
steps.detail: Every travel insurance policy has a 24×7 international emergency helpline. Save this number in your phone and carry it written on paper before you travel.

steps.title: Report Incident Within 24 Hours
steps.detail: For medical emergencies, trip cancellations, or theft — report to the insurer's helpline within 24 hours. Delayed reporting can lead to claim rejection.

steps.title: Collect All Evidence and Documents
steps.detail: For medical: get itemized bills and reports. For theft: get police FIR within 24 hours of incident. For flight delay: get airline's delay certificate. Evidence collected at the time is irreplaceable.

steps.title: For Medical Emergencies — Cashless if Available
steps.detail: Many travel insurers have a network of hospitals abroad. If cashless is available, present your policy details to the hospital. They coordinate with the insurer directly.

steps.title: Submit Claim on Return (or Online While Abroad)
steps.detail: Submit all documents along with the filled claim form. Most insurers accept online submissions. Deadline is typically 30–45 days from return date.

steps.title: Settlement in INR
steps.detail: Settlement is done in INR at the exchange rate applicable on the date of incident or settlement date (check policy). Direct bank transfer to your account.

claimTypes.timeline: Notification: Within 24 hours of incident. Document submission: 30–45 days after return. Settlement: 30 days from receipt of complete documents.
mistakes: Not saving the insurer's emergency helpline before travelling
Not reading the policy exclusions — pre-existing conditions, adventure sports, war zones are typically excluded
Buying too late — many policies don't cover events that arise after purchase (check terms)
Not filing a police FIR for theft or loss — insurer will reject without it
Losing flight delay certificates at the airport — ask airlines specifically for written delay certificates
Not declaring pre-existing conditions at time of purchase
tips: Buy travel insurance the day you book your tickets — covers trip cancellation from day 1
Screenshot your policy and save the emergency number on your phone
For senior travellers: ensure the policy covers pre-existing conditions explicitly
Adventure sports (trekking, scuba, skiing) require specific add-on cover — don't assume
Check the sum insured for medical expenses — minimum $50,000 recommended for USA/Europe
Keep a written note of policy number and helpline in your wallet separate from phone
